title: Why we post our losses
_note: EVERGREEN (warm-up bank for ad traffic). No invented numbers/prices/dates.
body:
🧠 Why we post our losses

Most channels show you only green. We show you red on purpose. Here's why that's not a weakness — it's the whole point.

Nobody is right every time
Leveraged futures is a game of being wrong often and managing it well. Anyone claiming an unbroken streak is either lying or deleting. A feed with zero losses isn't a feed of a great trader. It's a feed with the receipts removed.

What a red trade proves
When we post a loss, it comes with the SL that did its job. That's the system working out loud:
• The stop was set before entry.
• The size was decided before the click.
• When price hit invalidation, the position closed — controlled, not catastrophic.
A clean red is evidence our process is intact. A hidden red is evidence someone's hoping you won't ask.

The trust trade
We'd rather you trust us because you've SEEN us lose and watched the loss stay small, than trust a fantasy that breaks the first week you put real size behind it. Showing red costs us a little shine. It buys you a real picture of what trading with discipline actually looks like — green weeks and red ones, all on the record.

The quiet rule
If a channel never shows you a losing call, that's not confidence. That's the one thing you most need to see, missing. Ask where the losses went. The answer tells you everything.

We keep the whole record because the whole record is the proof. Green is easy to post. Red is the part that builds your trust.

#MindOverMarket
